timeline.story_start: Late August 2025 at the start of Haviland's fall semester, after Mohit loses a federal AI center bid and after Ruderal Analytics LLC is filed on August 14, 2025.
timeline.estimated_span: Late August 2025 through early summer 2026, with Leela's defense pushed beyond the narrated period into late summer or early fall.

key_events.event: Ruderal Analytics LLC is filed with Mohit as managing member.
Leela finds cloned lab repositories under Ruderal on Hades.
Omar publicly surfaces June's split flaw at seminar.
Research Integrity suspends Leela's privileged Hades access.
In Baltimore, June and Leela discover `ruderal_pitch_v3.pdf` and later sleep together.
Mohit formalizes one travel slot and one defense path; Omar later uses the smoke-run baseline.
Omar's funding letter is falsely signed and uploaded through Mohit's portal before the deadline.
A weakened grievance is assembled and Ben forwards it to Mohit.
Leela's pre-defense review defers scheduling without a new date.
Leela encrypts `holdout_set`; Marisol Vega preserves checksummed copies with a manifest and restricted custody for Omar's status-sensitive files.
key_events.timing: March 2026, immediately after the pre-defense deferral.

key_events.event: June, Ben, and Leela publicly corroborate the Ruderal evidence during demo day; Gloria places Mohit on leave.

Professor Halliday takes provisional substitute supervision; Leela accepts Montrose's one-year teaching fellowship; June leaves for Mercer Metrics; Omar moves toward provisional co-advising with Rina Patel while bridge funding stays unresolved.

geography.primary_setting: Haviland University in Lakebridge, New York, a cold lake city where the engineering complex feels sealed off from the rest of town.

key_locations.name: Civic Signals Lab, Keating Engineering basement
key_locations.details: Windowless lab, salvaged furniture, fluorescent hum, server heat.

key_locations.name: Shared graduate office, Keating sixth floor
key_locations.details: Cubicles, one lake window, radiators, printer heat.

key_locations.name: June's Alder Street apartment
key_locations.details: Cramped second-floor walk-up used for Tuesday dinners and late-night coalition work.

key_locations.name: Leela's graduate-housing studio
key_locations.details: Thin-walled unit with erratic heat, cheap blinds, desk, mattress, paper stacks.

key_locations.name: Baines Hall
key_locations.details: Black-glass administrative building where every room sounds already documented.

key_locations.name: Harbor East Conference Hotel, Baltimore
key_locations.details: Cold business hotel where the Ruderal deck is discovered.

key_locations.name: Anita Krishnan's townhouse, Edison, New Jersey
key_locations.details: Crowded family home with pressure-cooker noise and practiced competence.

key_locations.name: North Lake footbridge
key_locations.details: Wind-exposed path over frozen water where Leela decides to preserve the archive.

key_locations.name: Graduate workers union office above the Alder Street pharmacy
key_locations.details: Small upstairs office where Marisol Vega manifests, checksums, and preserves `holdout_set`.

key_locations.name: Haviland Innovation Atrium
key_locations.details: Glass donor space where demo day becomes public exposure.

distances: Keating to June's apartment is about a fifteen-minute walk downhill through the immigrant-commercial strip.
Keating to Leela's studio is about ten minutes on foot behind the library.
Baines Hall is across the quad from Keating and feels like another regime.
The North Lake footbridge is about twenty minutes from the lab and feels longer in winter.
Edison is far enough from Lakebridge to make chapter 8 a real removal from campus.
The union office sits on the same Alder Street strip as June's neighborhood, above the pharmacy.

world_rules: Faculty discretion still governs compute, authorship, travel, letters, and defense timing even when policy sounds neutral.
Haviland's IP rules give the university and PI broad claims over grant-funded lab artifacts unless title is challenged in a stronger record.
F-1 students need current funding documentation, enrollment, and I-20 updates; delay itself is material danger.
Complaints survive on timestamps, logs, attachments, and corroborating witnesses; oral truth alone gets reframed as climate or misunderstanding.
Scarcity can be weaponized without overt rule breaking: one travel slot, one defense path, one delayed signature, one careful subject line.
Graduate housing ends 30 days after a successful defense or 60 days after loss of student status, depending on which office is speaking.
This is realistic contemporary academia with no speculative or supernatural elements.

power_structure.over: Leela Krishnan, June Park, Omar Elmasry, and Ben Haber
power_structure.mechanism: Advisor control over authorship, compute, funding letters, sponsor access, recommendations, and defense timing.

power_structure.over: Mohit Saran and the graduate students indirectly
power_structure.mechanism: Chair authority over committees, investigations, substitute supervision, external communications, and report language.

power_structure.over: Leela Krishnan and the lab's infrastructure
power_structure.mechanism: Can suspend access, preserve repositories, and convert logs into record.

power_structure.holder: International Student Services and grant accounting
power_structure.mechanism: Require current funding letters, active appointments, and valid I-20 paperwork for status continuity.
Money and reputational pressure encourage containment until evidence becomes public and aligned.

power_structure.holder: Graduate workers union counsel and organizers
power_structure.mechanism: Manifested copies, encrypted storage, and timed release instructions limit how easily Haviland can narrow or quietly bury the archive.

objects.name: Hades clone logs and `hades-admin` trail
objects.origin: Existing lab compute system and inherited privileged credentials maintained informally inside the lab.
objects.status: Reveal September clone activity into Ruderal, later trigger Leela's access suspension, and become part of `holdout_set` and the preservation hold.

objects.name: `ruderal_pitch_v3.pdf`
objects.origin: Color sponsor deck created on November 12, 2025 (2:14 a.m. created; 7:06 a.m. modified) for Baltimore side meetings, using unpublished student figures and draft labels.
objects.status: Discovered in Baltimore, photographed, and later used in the grievance packet, `holdout_set`, and demo-day exposure.

objects.name: June's yellow legal pad
objects.origin: Private handwritten record of Mohit's shifting instructions and dates.
objects.status: Scanned into the grievance packet, quoted publicly in chapter 17, and retained privately with the archive.

objects.name: Omar's spring 2026 funding letter
objects.origin: Faculty certification required for January 15-August 31, 2026 funding, fee remission, and insurance proof.
objects.status: Edited, falsely signed from an earlier scan, and uploaded through Mohit's portal in chapter 12; later preserved as evidence of leverage.

objects.name: `holdout_set`
objects.owner: Leela Krishnan, June Park, and Omar Elmasry; preserved copies sit with union counsel and a separate graduate-union release path
objects.origin: Encrypted archive combining September logs, deck materials, grievance drafts, June's notes, Omar's letter trail, and dirty/clean paper versions.
objects.status: Checksummed and manifested with union counsel in chapter 16; Nina Solis holds a backup release path for demo day; the archive remains privately held after the narrowed university report in chapter 18.

debts.amount: About $4,800 revolving balance in Leela's name
debts.deadline: Minimum payment due monthly on the eighteenth
debts.status: Anita's $1,200 transfer eases immediate pressure in chapter 8, but the debt remains active until Leela has the Montrose salary bridge in chapter 18.

income_sources.source: Leela's Haviland doctoral stipend and teaching assignment
income_sources.reliability: Stable only while her student status and bridge remain intact.
Stable through the academic year; replaced by Mercer Metrics salary after chapter 18 (amount unspecified).

income_sources.source: Omar's stipend, fee remission, and health insurance through lab funding
income_sources.amount: About $36,000 annualized plus tuition, mandatory fees, and insurance coverage
income_sources.reliability: Administratively fragile because it depends on current funding letters, valid I-20 paperwork, and later bridge funding.
Accepted in chapter 18; start paperwork depends on degree/ABD status, and benefits begin September 1.

knowledge_state.knows: Mohit cloned student repositories into a private Ruderal directory on Hades.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: June until chapter_13; Omar until chapter_06/chapter_13; the administration until later archive/public exposure.

knowledge_state.knows: Mohit staged June's seminar ambush and tied Omar's visibility to playing the internal adversary.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: June until after the seminar; outsiders until later grievance work.

knowledge_state.knows: Leela says the defense is still on track for March or early April.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Both are kept from the real committee instability and later deferral.

knowledge_state.hidden_from: Mohit until chapter_17; outside witnesses until demo day.

knowledge_state.knows: Ben is relaying sponsor-deck and provenance chatter back to Mohit.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Ben is never confronted cleanly before chapter_14.

knowledge_state.knows: Omar's immigration paperwork will become dangerous if his funding letter is delayed.
Leela withheld the September server evidence and dirty-paper trail from the grievance packet.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Department offices and committee packets until the union archive preserves the full record.

knowledge_state.knows: Leela's fall workshop robustness claim did not replicate cleanly under rerun.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Committees and outside readers until he weaponizes it in chapter_15.

knowledge_state.character: Former Civic Signals Lab students, union counsel, and Nina Solis

knowledge_state.knows: Mohit offered a Brooklyn model-evaluation off-ramp if she stopped attaching herself to the grievance.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Outside offices until June later recounts the offer; Mohit presents it as a private career favor.

knowledge_state.knows: The final findings will use donor-safe language about disclosure failures, authorship misrepresentation, entrepreneurial overlap, and possible advising conduct rather than the students' harsher terms.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Sponsors and public readers until the narrowed report language circulates.

environmental_constants: The basement lab always smells of burnt coffee, hot dust, old upholstery, and server heat.
Keating's fluorescent lights hum hard enough that silence there is never actual silence.
Lakebridge winter arrives first as wet salt and damp wool before clean snow or solid lake ice.
Graduate housing radiators clang unpredictably, especially at night.
Anita's Edison house smells of hot oil, sandalwood, spices, and winter things drying by the door.
The Innovation Atrium is polished glass built to project transparency while amplifying performance.
Baines Hall smells of artificial citrus cleaner and vacuumed carpet.
